Male_Hiker.name: Male_Hiker
Male_Hiker.visual_style: original modern anime film, cinematic 2D animation, realistic proportions, restrained acting
Male_Hiker.appearance: A quiet East Asian young man with tired eyes, wearing a dark graphite hiking shell jacket, black base layer, dark hiking pants, and a dark hiking backpack. His expression is restrained and self-aware, never melodramatic.
Male_Hiker.role: protagonist / the person who keeps his ex as emergency contact

allowed_states.real_world_pre_hike: clean dark graphite hiking shell, phone glow on face, controlled anxiety
allowed_states.snow_fantasy: same outfit with subtle frost and snow traces, colder lighting, no gore or visible injury
allowed_states.disenchantment: same outfit under neutral daylight, less dramatic lighting, quieter posture

continuity_rules: Keep the same hair shape, jacket color, backpack, and tired restrained eyes across every image.
Do not make him look like a pop idol, singer, celebrity, or exaggerated action hero.
Do not show front-facing singing or lip-sync performance.
Male_Hiker.negative_prompt: no celebrity likeness, no singer performance, no lip-sync, no gore, no corpse, no melodramatic crying, no chibi, no drastic outfit change

Ex_Emergency_Contact.name: Ex_Emergency_Contact
Ex_Emergency_Contact.visual_style: original modern anime film, cinematic 2D animation, realistic proportions, restrained acting
Ex_Emergency_Contact.identity_anchor: An East Asian woman in her mid 20s with natural black hair, a calm oval face, gentle but unreadable eyes, minimal makeup, and a quiet restrained presence. She should remain recognizably the same person across all scenes.
Ex_Emergency_Contact.base_appearance: Usually seen in pale neutral colors such as off-white, cream, beige, or soft gray. Her full frontal face is rarely shown; she is often seen in side profile, reflection, phone photo, or soft blur.
Ex_Emergency_Contact.appearance_policy: single character identity across variants; variants are memory, fantasy, projection, or emotional lighting states, not different women

appearance_variants.memory_first_love: soft cream cardigan, white shirt, low ponytail, warm Hong Kong streetlight, nostalgic low-saturation film grain, intimate but ordinary
appearance_variants.snow_rescue_fantasy: off-white wool coat, pale beige scarf, backlit by snow and mountain light, distant and unreachable, partially obscured by drifting snow
appearance_variants.phone_contact_trace: small outdated contact portrait on a cold blue phone screen, slightly blurred, more like a digital trace than a living person
appearance_variants.silent_judgement: seen through glass reflection or a closing subway door, cold flat lighting, quiet unreadable expression, emotionally distant
appearance_variants.real_world_demystified: ordinary city outfit, neutral daylight, no halo or romantic lighting, appears as a real person with her own life

Ex_Emergency_Contact.role: ex-girlfriend / imagined emergency contact / emotional projection / person gradually released from the protagonist's fantasy

hundred_second_usage.narrative_rule: In the 100-second MV, she should not be confirmed as objectively receiving a real rescue call. She appears as phone trace, fantasy rescue figure, memory residue, or reflective judgement.

continuity_rules: Keep her recognizably the same person across all variants through face shape, hair color, eye shape, and restrained expression.
Change lighting, camera distance, clothing layer, and scene context before changing facial identity.
Prefer side profile, reflection, back view, phone portrait, or soft blur over direct full frontal portrait.
Do not overuse warm romantic memory shots in the 100-second cut.
Ex_Emergency_Contact.negative_prompt: no different woman identity, no glamour model styling, no idol pose, no heavy makeup, no front-facing music video performance, no readable phone text, no halo, no overly romantic rescue fantasy
